title: Rahm's Masterclass: Legion XIII Sweeps Mexico City
excerpt: Jon Rahm delivers a six-shot victory in Mexico City with a bogey-free final round, as Legion XIII claims both individual and team titles.
body:
Rahm Takes Command in Mexico

Jon Rahm closed out LIV Golf Mexico City with a masterclass on Sunday, carding a flawless 7-under 64 to claim a commanding six-shot victory over David Puig. The Legion XIII captain's dominant performance was a complete reversal from his disappointing tie for 38th at Augusta just seven days earlier — and it sent a clear message to the rest of the league: Rahm is in ruthless form heading into the business end of the season.

Winning Margin
6 shots
Final Score
-21
Final Round Score
7-under 64
Rahm's Career LIV Wins
4

The Four-Hole Dominance

The tournament was essentially decided in a four-hole stretch early in Rahm's final round. After opening with a par, he drove the 318-yard second hole and two-putted from 66 feet for birdie. He then drove the 390-yard third green and calmly converted from 3 feet for eagle. Birdies on the fourth and fifth holes extended his lead to an insurmountable six shots. By the time his playing partner Tyrrell Hatton suffered a double bogey at that same drivable second hole, the contest was over.

The Spanish Podium

Spain owned the top three finishes at Chapultepec. After Rahm's commanding victory, the 24-year-old David Puig claimed runner-up honours with a career-best -15 finish, whilst 22-year-old Josele Ballester rounded out the podium at -14. The trio spend enormous amounts of practice time together — Rahm himself quipped that he spends more time with Puig than his own family — and their domination underscores the depth of current Spanish golf talent.

Record-Breaking Trophies

With both the individual title and team trophy (Legion XIII won by nine shots), Rahm became LIV Golf's all-time trophy leader with 16 total honours — moving one ahead of Bryson DeChambeau despite playing two fewer seasons. The stat speaks to his versatility: four tournament wins, two Individual Championships, and a Team Championship demonstrate a player who excels across every format the league offers.

High-Altitude Spectacle

Club de Golf Chapultepec's elevation continued to produce fireworks. Six 400-yard drives were recorded Sunday, with Elvis Smylie launching a 435.5-yard monster at the 13th hole — the longest of the week. The altitude, combined with Rahm's impeccable ballstriking, made for compelling theatre. Meanwhile, Thomas Detry's tie for fifth has positioned him perfectly for a potential U.S. Open exemption, depending on the Individual standings after Virginia in early May.
